title: Claw Care 101: Spring Maintenance for Healthy Kits
description: Essential tips for maintaining your kit's claws during the spring shedding season in the Northern Hemisphere.
main_content: Spring is here, and with it comes shedding season for many kits. This is the perfect time to focus on claw health. Start by checking for overgrowth or damage. Use a soft brush to remove loose fur around the claw beds. Provide a scratching post or rough surface for natural filing. For indoor kits, consider nail trimming every 2-3 weeks, but avoid cutting the quick. Moisturize paw pads with pet-safe balm to prevent cracking. Monitor for signs of infection like redness or swelling. A healthy claw routine now prevents summer issues.
key_takeaways: Inspect claws weekly during spring shedding.
Provide scratching surfaces for natural maintenance.
Trim nails carefully to avoid the quick.
Moisturize paw pads to prevent dryness.
